course.title: SQL Database Fundamentals
course.description: Learn SQL to query, manage, and analyze data in relational databases.

chapter.title: Modifying Data
chapter.description: Learn how to insert new data, update existing data, and delete data.

lessons.title: Inserting New Rows
objectives: Add new records to a table using INSERT INTO
lessons.explanation: The `INSERT INTO` statement is used to insert new records in a table.
lessons.syntax: INSERT INTO table_name (column1, column2) VALUES (value1, value2);

exercises.statement: Insert a new row into the `employees` table with an id of 4, name 'David', and role 'Designer'. Then SELECT all rows to verify.
exercises.hint: Use INSERT INTO employees (id, name, role) VALUES (4, 'David', 'Designer'); followed by a SELECT query.
exercises.solution:
INSERT INTO employees (id, name, role) VALUES (4, 'David', 'Designer');
SELECT * FROM employees;

lessons.title: Updating Existing Rows
lessons.explanation: The `UPDATE` statement is used to modify the existing records in a table. Be careful! If you omit the WHERE clause, ALL records will be updated!
lessons.syntax: UPDATE table_name SET column1 = value1 WHERE condition;

exercises.statement: Update the `employees` table to change Alice's role to 'Senior Engineer'. Her id is 1. Then SELECT all rows to verify.
exercises.hint: Use UPDATE employees SET role = 'Senior Engineer' WHERE id = 1;
exercises.solution:
UPDATE employees SET role = 'Senior Engineer' WHERE id = 1;
SELECT * FROM employees;
